## Usage Examples

### Example 1: Basic Subscription with Automatic Commits

```java
public class AutoCommitConsumer {
    public static void main(String[] args) {
        Properties props = new Properties();
        props.setProperty("bootstrap.servers", "localhost:9092");
        props.setProperty("group.id", "my-group");
        props.setProperty("enable.auto.commit", "true");
        props.setProperty("auto.commit.interval.ms", "1000");
        props.setProperty("key.deserializer", "org.apache.kafka.common.serialization.StringDeserializer");
        props.setProperty("value.deserializer", "org.apache.kafka.common.serialization.StringDeserializer");

        KafkaConsumer<String, String> consumer = new KafkaConsumer<>(props);
        consumer.subscribe(Arrays.asList("my-topic"));

        try {
            while (true) {
                ConsumerRecords<String, String> records = consumer.poll(Duration.ofMillis(100));
                for (ConsumerRecord<String, String> record : records) {
                    System.out.printf(
                        "offset = %d, key = %s, value = %s%n",
                        record.offset(),
                        record.key(),
                        record.value()
                    );
                }
            }
        } finally {
            consumer.close();
        }
    }
}
```

### Example 2: Manual Offset Control with Batch Processing

```java
public class ManualCommitConsumer {
    public static void main(String[] args) {
        Properties props = new Properties();
        props.setProperty("bootstrap.servers", "localhost:9092");
        props.setProperty("group.id", "batch-group");
        props.setProperty("enable.auto.commit", "false");  // Manual commits
        props.setProperty("key.deserializer", "org.apache.kafka.common.serialization.StringDeserializer");
        props.setProperty("value.deserializer", "org.apache.kafka.common.serialization.StringDeserializer");

        KafkaConsumer<String, String> consumer = new KafkaConsumer<>(props);
        consumer.subscribe(Arrays.asList("my-topic"));

        List<ConsumerRecord<String, String>> buffer = new ArrayList<>();
        int batchSize = 100;

        try {
            while (true) {
                ConsumerRecords<String, String> records = consumer.poll(Duration.ofMillis(100));

                for (ConsumerRecord<String, String> record : records) {
                    buffer.add(record);
                }

                // Batch processing
                if (buffer.size() >= batchSize) {
                    processBatch(buffer);
                    buffer.clear();

                    // Commit only after successful processing
                    try {
                        consumer.commitSync();
                        System.out.println("Offsets committed");
                    } catch (CommitFailedException e) {
                        System.err.println("Commit failed - consumer lost partitions");
                        break;
                    }
                }
            }
        } finally {
            consumer.close();
        }
    }

    static void processBatch(List<ConsumerRecord<String, String>> records) {
        // Simulate batch processing
        System.out.println("Processing batch of " + records.size() + " records");
    }
}
```

### Example 3: Rebalance Listener with Custom Offset Management

```java
public class RebalanceListenerExample {
    static class SaveOffsetsOnRebalance implements ConsumerRebalanceListener {
        private KafkaConsumer<String, String> consumer;
        private Map<TopicPartition, Long> offsetStore = new HashMap<>();

        public SaveOffsetsOnRebalance(KafkaConsumer<String, String> consumer) {
            this.consumer = consumer;
        }

        @Override
        public void onPartitionsRevoked(Collection<TopicPartition> partitions) {
            // Save offsets to external store
            for (TopicPartition partition : partitions) {
                long offset = consumer.position(partition);
                offsetStore.put(partition, offset);
                System.out.println("Revoked " + partition + " at offset " + offset);
            }
        }

        @Override
        public void onPartitionsAssigned(Collection<TopicPartition> partitions) {
            // Restore offsets from external store
            for (TopicPartition partition : partitions) {
                Long offset = offsetStore.getOrDefault(partition, 0L);
                consumer.seek(partition, offset);
                System.out.println("Assigned " + partition + " at offset " + offset);
            }
        }

        @Override
        public void onPartitionsLost(Collection<TopicPartition> partitions) {
            // Partitions lost without revoke - don't save offsets
            System.out.println("Lost partitions: " + partitions);
        }
    }

    public static void main(String[] args) {
        Properties props = new Properties();
        props.setProperty("bootstrap.servers", "localhost:9092");
        props.setProperty("group.id", "rebalance-group");
        props.setProperty("enable.auto.commit", "false");
        props.setProperty("key.deserializer", "org.apache.kafka.common.serialization.StringDeserializer");
        props.setProperty("value.deserializer", "org.apache.kafka.common.serialization.StringDeserializer");

        KafkaConsumer<String, String> consumer = new KafkaConsumer<>(props);
        ConsumerRebalanceListener rebalanceListener = new SaveOffsetsOnRebalance(consumer);

        consumer.subscribe(Arrays.asList("my-topic"), rebalanceListener);

        try {
            while (true) {
                ConsumerRecords<String, String> records = consumer.poll(Duration.ofMillis(100));
                for (ConsumerRecord<String, String> record : records) {
                    System.out.println("Processing: " + record.value());
                }

                // Commit current offsets
                if (records.count() > 0) {
                    consumer.commitSync();
                }
            }
        } catch (WakeupException e) {
            // Shutdown
        } finally {
            consumer.close();
        }
    }
}
```

### Example 4: Partition-Specific Commits

```java
public class PartitionSpecificCommit {
    public static void main(String[] args) {
        Properties props = new Properties();
        props.setProperty("bootstrap.servers", "localhost:9092");
        props.setProperty("group.id", "partition-group");
        props.setProperty("enable.auto.commit", "false");
        props.setProperty("key.deserializer", "org.apache.kafka.common.serialization.StringDeserializer");
        props.setProperty("value.deserializer", "org.apache.kafka.common.serialization.StringDeserializer");

        KafkaConsumer<String, String> consumer = new KafkaConsumer<>(props);
        consumer.subscribe(Arrays.asList("my-topic"));

        try {
            while (true) {
                ConsumerRecords<String, String> records = consumer.poll(Duration.ofMillis(100));

                // Process records and commit per partition
                for (TopicPartition partition : records.partitions()) {
                    List<ConsumerRecord<String, String>> partitionRecords = records.records(partition);

                    for (ConsumerRecord<String, String> record : partitionRecords) {
                        System.out.println("Processing: " + record.value());
                    }

                    // Commit the next offset for this partition
                    long nextOffset = partitionRecords.get(partitionRecords.size() - 1).offset() + 1;
                    consumer.commitSync(
                        Collections.singletonMap(
                            partition,
                            new OffsetAndMetadata(nextOffset)
                        )
                    );
                }
            }
        } finally {
            consumer.close();
        }
    }
}

public class SeekExample {
    public static void main(String[] args) {
        Properties props = new Properties();
        props.setProperty("bootstrap.servers", "localhost:9092");
        props.setProperty("group.id", "seek-group");
        props.setProperty("key.deserializer", "org.apache.kafka.common.serialization.StringDeserializer");

        KafkaConsumer<String, String> consumer = new KafkaConsumer<>(props);

        // Manual assignment instead of subscription
        TopicPartition partition = new TopicPartition("my-topic", 0);
        consumer.assign(Collections.singletonList(partition));

        // Seek to beginning
        consumer.seekToBeginning(Collections.singletonList(partition));

        // Or seek to specific offset
        consumer.seek(partition, 100);

        // Or seek to timestamp (5 minutes ago)
        long timestamp = System.currentTimeMillis() - 5 * 60 * 1000;
        Map<TopicPartition, OffsetAndTimestamp> offsets = consumer.offsetsForTimes(
            Collections.singletonMap(partition, timestamp)
        );
        if (offsets.get(partition) != null) {
            consumer.seek(partition, offsets.get(partition).offset());
        }

        try {
            while (true) {
                ConsumerRecords<String, String> records = consumer.poll(Duration.ofMillis(100));
                for (ConsumerRecord<String, String> record : records) {
                    System.out.printf(
                        "Offset: %d, Timestamp: %d, Value: %s%n",
                        record.offset(),
                        record.timestamp(),
                        record.value()
                    );
                }
            }
        } finally {
            consumer.close();
        }
    }
}

### Example 6: Multi-Threaded Processing with Wakeup

```java
public class MultiThreadedConsumer implements Runnable {
    private final KafkaConsumer<String, String> consumer;
    private final AtomicBoolean shutdown = new AtomicBoolean(false);
    private final ExecutorService executor = Executors.newFixedThreadPool(4);

    public MultiThreadedConsumer() {
        Properties props = new Properties();
        props.setProperty("bootstrap.servers", "localhost:9092");
        props.setProperty("group.id", "multithreaded-group");
        props.setProperty("enable.auto.commit", "false");
        props.setProperty("max.poll.records", "10");
        props.setProperty("key.deserializer", "org.apache.kafka.common.serialization.StringDeserializer");
        props.setProperty("value.deserializer", "org.apache.kafka.common.serialization.StringDeserializer");

        this.consumer = new KafkaConsumer<>(props);
    }

    @Override
    public void run() {
        try {
            consumer.subscribe(Arrays.asList("my-topic"));

            while (!shutdown.get()) {
                ConsumerRecords<String, String> records = consumer.poll(Duration.ofMillis(1000));

                // Submit records to thread pool for processing
                for (ConsumerRecord<String, String> record : records) {
                    executor.submit(() -> {
                        try {
                            processRecord(record);
                        } catch (Exception e) {
                            System.err.println("Processing failed: " + e.getMessage());
                        }
                    });
                }

                // Commit asynchronously
                if (records.count() > 0) {
                    consumer.commitAsync((offsets, exception) -> {
                        if (exception != null) {
                            System.err.println("Async commit failed: " + exception.getMessage());
                        }
                    });
                }
            }
        } catch (WakeupException e) {
            // Expected during shutdown
        } finally {
            try {
                // Wait for in-flight tasks
                executor.shutdown();
                executor.awaitTermination(10, TimeUnit.SECONDS);

                // Final sync commit
                consumer.commitSync();
            } catch (Exception e) {
                System.err.println("Shutdown error: " + e.getMessage());
            } finally {
                consumer.close();
            }
        }
    }

    private void processRecord(ConsumerRecord<String, String> record) {
        // Simulate processing
        Thread.sleep(10);
        System.out.println("Processed: " + record.value());
    }

    public void shutdown() {
        shutdown.set(true);
        consumer.wakeup();
    }
}
```

## Common Patterns and Best Practices

### Pattern 1: Graceful Shutdown

```java
public class GracefulShutdown {
    public static void main(String[] args) {
        KafkaConsumer<String, String> consumer = createConsumer();

        // Register shutdown hook
        Runtime.getRuntime().addShutdownHook(new Thread(() -> {
            System.out.println("Shutting down consumer");
            consumer.wakeup();
        }));

        try {
            while (true) {
                ConsumerRecords<String, String> records = consumer.poll(Duration.ofMillis(100));
                for (ConsumerRecord<String, String> record : records) {
                    processRecord(record);
                }
                consumer.commitAsync();
            }
        } catch (WakeupException e) {
            System.out.println("Consumer woken up");
        } finally {
            try {
                consumer.commitSync();  // Final sync commit
                System.out.println("Offsets committed");
            } catch (CommitFailedException e) {
                System.out.println("Final commit failed: " + e.getMessage());
            } finally {
                consumer.close();
            }
        }
    }
}
```

### Pattern 2: Exactly-Once Semantics with External State

```java
public class ExactlyOnceConsumer {
    private Database db;

    public void consume() {
        KafkaConsumer<String, String> consumer = createConsumer();

        try {
            while (true) {
                ConsumerRecords<String, String> records = consumer.poll(Duration.ofMillis(100));

                for (ConsumerRecord<String, String> record : records) {
                    // Atomic transaction: store result + offset
                    db.beginTransaction();
                    try {
                        processAndStore(record);
                        db.storeOffset(record.topic(), record.partition(), record.offset() + 1);
                        db.commit();
                    } catch (Exception e) {
                        db.rollback();
                        throw e;
                    }
                }
            }
        } finally {
            consumer.close();
        }
    }
}
```

### Pattern 3: Consumer Health Monitoring

```java
public class MonitoredConsumer {
    public static void main(String[] args) {
        KafkaConsumer<String, String> consumer = createConsumer();
        ScheduledExecutorService scheduler = Executors.newScheduledThreadPool(1);

        // Monitor lag and membership
        scheduler.scheduleAtFixedRate(() -> {
            for (TopicPartition tp : consumer.assignment()) {
                long position = consumer.position(tp);
                Map<TopicPartition, Long> endOffsets = consumer.endOffsets(
                    Collections.singletonList(tp)
                );
                long lag = endOffsets.get(tp) - position;

                OptionalLong currentLag = consumer.currentLag(tp);
                System.out.printf(
                    "%s: position=%d, lag=%d%n",
                    tp,
                    position,
                    currentLag.isPresent() ? currentLag.getAsLong() : lag
                );
            }
        }, 0, 10, TimeUnit.SECONDS);

        try {
            while (true) {
                ConsumerRecords<String, String> records = consumer.poll(Duration.ofMillis(100));
                for (ConsumerRecord<String, String> record : records) {
                    processRecord(record);
                }
                consumer.commitAsync();
            }
        } finally {
            scheduler.shutdown();
            consumer.close();
        }
    }
}
```

### Pattern 4: Slow Processing with Pause/Resume

```java
public class PauseResumePattern {
    public static void main(String[] args) {
        KafkaConsumer<String, String> consumer = createConsumer();
        ExecutorService executor = Executors.newFixedThreadPool(1);
        Future<?> processingTask = null;
        boolean paused = false;

        try {
            while (true) {
                ConsumerRecords<String, String> records = consumer.poll(Duration.ofMillis(100));

                if (!records.isEmpty() && processingTask == null) {
                    // Pause to avoid receiving more records
                    consumer.pause(consumer.assignment());
                    paused = true;

                    // Submit batch for processing
                    processingTask = executor.submit(() -> {
                        processBatch(records);
                    });
                } else if (processingTask != null && processingTask.isDone()) {
                    // Resume when batch processing completes
                    processingTask = null;
                    consumer.resume(consumer.assignment());
                    paused = false;
                }

                // Commit when processing done
                if (processingTask != null && processingTask.isDone()) {
                    consumer.commitAsync();
                }
            }
        } finally {
            if (paused) {
                consumer.resume(consumer.assignment());
            }
            executor.shutdown();
            consumer.close();
        }
    }
}
```

### Pattern 5: Regex Subscription with Pattern Monitoring

```java
public class RegexSubscriptionPattern {
    public static void main(String[] args) {
        KafkaConsumer<String, String> consumer = createConsumer();

        // Subscribe to topics matching pattern
        consumer.subscribe(Pattern.compile("events-.*"));

        ScheduledExecutorService scheduler = Executors.newScheduledThreadPool(1);

        // Monitor new matching topics
        scheduler.scheduleAtFixedRate(() -> {
            Set<String> subscribed = consumer.subscription();
            System.out.println("Currently subscribed to topics: " + subscribed);
        }, 0, 30, TimeUnit.SECONDS);

        try {
            while (true) {
                ConsumerRecords<String, String> records = consumer.poll(Duration.ofMillis(100));

                for (ConsumerRecord<String, String> record : records) {
                    System.out.printf(
                        "Topic: %s, Offset: %d, Value: %s%n",
                        record.topic(),
                        record.offset(),
                        record.value()
                    );
                }

                consumer.commitAsync();
            }
        } finally {
            scheduler.shutdown();
            consumer.close();
        }
    }
}
```

### Best Practices Summary

1. **Always use rebalance listeners with subscriptions** for proper offset management
2. **Keep the poll loop tight** - poll frequently, process records asynchronously
3. **Prefer `commitSync()` after processing** for safety; use `commitAsync()` for throughput
4. **Never hold partitions without polling** - consumer will be removed from group
5. **Use `pause()`/`resume()` for flow control**, not `unsubscribe()`
6. **Implement proper shutdown** with `wakeup()` and final `commitSync()`
7. **Monitor lag and rebalancing** for operational visibility
8. **Handle `CommitFailedException`** gracefully - it signals loss of group membership
9. **Use `read_committed` isolation level** for transactional safety
10. **Test rebalancing behavior** - ensure callbacks are correct
11. **Consider external offset storage** for exactly-once semantics
12. **Size batches for `max.poll.interval.ms`** to avoid removal from group

---

## Summary of Key Behavioral Semantics

### Offset Semantics
- **Position** (next to fetch) vs **Committed** (stored for restart)
- Committed offset should be **next offset to read** (one past last processed)
- `ConsumerRecords.nextOffsets()` provides the correct committed offset

### Rebalancing Semantics
- Only detected/executed during `poll()`
- Callbacks run synchronously within `poll()` call
- `onPartitionsRevoked()` called before partition migration
- `onPartitionsAssigned()` called after acquisition
- Can block arbitrary duration during rebalance

### Group Membership
- Join happens on first `poll()`, not during `subscribe()`
- Proactive leave if `poll()` not called within `max.poll.interval.ms`
- Heartbeats sent independently by background thread
- Group coordinator can change, triggering rebalance

### Isolation Levels
- **read_uncommitted**: All messages (including in-flight transactions)
- **read_committed**: Only committed messages, read up to LSO

### Error Recovery
- `CommitFailedException`: Fatal - consumer lost group membership
- `RecordDeserializationException`: Non-fatal - record skipped
- `WakeupException`: Expected on shutdown - not an error
- Transient errors are retried internally
